description: Account for burial expenses. Goitein (Med. Soc., 4:160) interprets the deceased as belonging to the lower middle class, but he may have been affluent: in addition to a tunic, two robes, a cloak, a scarf, and numerous porters and laborers, there may also be a mausoleum involved. There is also a cost for making the trek through the desert to the cemetery. NB דואמיס are trestles, per Goitein and Blau, on which to rest the coffin or bier both at the cemetery and en route. For additional burial accounts, see Bodl. MS heb. f 103/40 (PGPID 2490) and T-S Ar.4.5 (PGPID 1259), the will of al-Wuḥsha al-Dallāla.
description_he: רשימה או טבלה. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
